name: Jack DeJohnette
description: American jazz drummer, pianist, and composer (1942–2025)
abstract: Jack DeJohnette (August 9, 1942 – October 26, 2025) was an American jazz drummer, pianist and composer. Known for his extensive work as leader and sideman for musicians including Charles Lloyd, Freddie Hubbard, Keith Jarrett, Bill Evans, John Abercrombie, Ralph Towner, Alice Coltrane, Sonny Rollins, Miles Davis, Joe Henderson, Michael Br…

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: Jack formed a band with myself and Alex Foster... And we toured for a while as a band and made a couple of records for Manfred — Manfred, I say Manfred — ECM.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Terri Lyne Carrington
about_quotes.evidence: That’s why Jack DeJohnette is one of my favorite drummers and has been a mentor to me over the years
about_quotes.doc_title: Terri Lyne Carrington interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: I just recorded with him last month as a matter of fact with a Japanese alto player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: this is Jack DeJohnette, Keith Jarrett, myself and Charles Lloyd.

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] So on this album Inner Voices, some tracks have Eric Gravatt, some tracks have Jack DeJohnette... [INTERVIEWEE] I had to fire the left hand because it was dominating (laughs). I’m sorry, but I like to laugh. He’s a good guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: Jack DeJohnette was just arriving into New York from Chicago; and uh, we quickly groomed him for the trio behind Abbey Lincoln. Abbey Lincoln and the Cedar Walton Trio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
